narrative: Consumer discretionary and technology each added 2.4 percent today, a notable one-day reversal that pushes back against the defensive tone that has dominated the five-day picture, where healthcare, utilities, and staples have been the clear leaders. With only one of eleven sectors in the red and credit spreads sitting at historically tight levels, the session reads as a tentative reassertion of growth appetite rather than a sustained shift. Watch whether discretionary and technology can hold these gains into the week's close, as a fade back toward defensives would suggest today's move is positioning noise rather than the start of a genuine rotation.
